ogTitle: St Helens Plains vs St James: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in St Helens Plains and St James.
heading: St Helens Plains vs St James — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between St Helens Plains, Victoria and St James, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: St James has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to St Helens Plains (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is St Helens Plains or St James safer from flooding?
faq.answer: St Helens Plains has a flood risk score of 24/100, while St James scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: St Helens Plains scores 63/100 for bushfire risk. St James scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in St Helens Plains or St James?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. St James has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
